patches.find: undermining this as a genuine dome-physics prediction. Given SAA recurrence intervals of ~200–400 years (Hare et al. 2018) and archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty of ±50–100 years, the 50-year gap between ~950 AD and the theological ~1000 AD target falls within noise — any of several recurrences could have been claimed as a match. The dome's own evolution notes admit it "completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics," further undermining this as a genuine dome-physics prediction.
patches.replace: undermining this as a genuine dome-physics prediction.
patches.rationale: ISS-548 (moderate): WIN-023 detail_evidence ends with a duplicate paragraph. ISS-539 patch added the recurrence text but it was already present, creating an exact duplicate. Remove the second instance. The text 'Given SAA recurrence intervals...' appears twice; this patch removes the trailing duplicate copy.

patches.find: Geomagnetic excursion ~950 CE is documented in archaeomagnetic records (Campuzano 2019) — the timing is real and not disputed; the dome’s failure is the unfalsifiable theological overlay connecting this to Schumann changes, and the absence of any dome-derived mechanism
patches.replace: Real paleomagnetic date (Campuzano 2019) adopted post-hoc; theological overlay without dome-derived mechanism
patches.rationale: ISS-549 (minor): WIN-023 finding field is 269 chars — a full paragraph that dominates the summary table. ISS-538 patch was written in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T23-45.json but was not applied to wins.json. Re-issuing: shorten to a concise one-liner (~80 chars) that is readable in the summary table.

patches.find: Adding free parameters (quadratic term) always increases R²; no AIC or BIC correction penalizes overfitting.
patches.replace: Adding free parameters (quadratic term) always increases R²; no AIC or BIC correction penalizes overfitting. The dome model's own evolution history provides the most decisive evidence: raw-text/05-model.txt records that V50.4 introduced the quadratic as a correction after the circular disc geometry failed (‘v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances — That was correct’), then V50.6 superseded the quadratic with the exponential firmament function H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619), claiming 5.2% mean error — implicitly retiring the quadratic. A ‘confirmed WIN’ that the dome itself has abandoned for a successor mechanism is post-hoc iterative parameterisation, not a genuine prediction. The 20 flight/shipping routes the dome claims to have measured are never specified in any data file or script: find_curve.py contains only 6 hardcoded city-pair distances and an empty regression function (a bare pass statement). Without the route list and wind corrections documented, the R²=0.7874 claim is an unreproducible assertion. With 3 free parameters and only 20 claimed observations, the Adjusted R² ≈ 0.75 — overfitting is substantial; cross-validation would likely reduce this further.
patches.rationale: ISS-550/551/552 combined (critical + moderate + minor): WIN-027 Cycle 2 review identified three closely related gaps in the evidence. (1) CRITICAL: the quadratic was superseded by H(r) in V50.6 — the dome model has internally abandoned it yet it remains a 'WIN'. (2) MODERATE: the 20 claimed routes are never documented; find_curve.py has only 6 hardcoded cities and an empty function. (3) MINOR: Adjusted R² with N=20, k=3 is ≈0.75 — overfitting is severe. These three arguments are combined into one evidence-block addition since they naturally flow together. WIN-027.c2.json reviewed 2026-04-07T14:45:30Z.

ISS-553 (code_analysis): WIN-027 Cycle 2 confirms monitoring tag should be 'hardcoded', not 'live_fetch'. The C1 tag was incorrect. No script in the dome repository performs live data fetches for WIN-027. The quadratic coefficients (0.00131, 0.06828, 1.06719) and R²=0.7874 appear only as static assertions in raw-text files and wins.html display. The find_curve.py skeleton has 6 hardcoded cities and an empty regression function. 'live_fetch' misrepresents dome infrastructure. Alternatively: node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace
